channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House Rep. Eli Crane says therapy dogs should be mandatory at hearings after joke about Dems' mental stability
item.description: U.S. Rep. Eli Crane, R-Ariz., on Thursday said he felt better when he saw three beagles in a committee hearing after joking about Democrats a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department appeals as second judge in 2 days blocks Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A second federal judge in two days has blocked President Donald Trump’s effort to end birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally, decrying what he described as the administration's attempt to ignore the Constitution for political gain. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump promotes misleading claims about federal government’s media subscriptions
item.description: Federal government payments for subscriptions to media outlets have become the latest target of President Donald Trump's spending hawks.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal workers need more time on buyout decision: Money expert
item.description: Millions of U.S. government employees must make a decision whether to accept a buyout offer from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs order imposing sanctions on International Criminal Court over investigations of Israel
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order imposing sanctions on the International Criminal Court over investigations of Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOGE was tasked with stopping Treasury payments to USAID, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Officials working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency sought access to the U.S. Department of Treasury payment system to stop money from flowing to the U.S. Agency for International Development, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Justice Department ends Biden-era task force aimed at seizing assets of Russian oligarchs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's Justice Department has disbanded a Biden-era program aimed at seizing the assets of Russian oligarchs as a means to punish Russia for its invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems who lashed out at Trump for being 'threat to democracy' flip script with last-minute delay tactics
item.description: Voters overwhelmingly elected President Donald Trump to carry out his agenda despite Democrats calling him a "threat to democracy." Dems are now delaying key cabinet appointments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House passes bill to permanently classify fentanyl as Schedule 1 drug
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers in the House passed the HALT Act on Thursday, a bill that seeks to permanently classify fentanyl as a Schedule 1 substance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas under partial hiring freeze as city readies for Badlands deal
item.description: Las Vegas expects a settlement soon in litigation over the Badlands golf course, and has already begun to freeze hiring to save money, according to city officials.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: More of Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Some are already at work.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IRS workers involved in 2025 tax season can't take buyout until after the taxpayer filing deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — IRS employees involved in the 2025 tax season will not be allowed to accept a buyout offer from the Trump administration until after the taxpayer filing deadline, according to a letter sent Wednesday to IRS employees.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Judge temporarily blocks Trump plan offering incentives for federal workers to resign
item.description: A federal judge has blocked President Donald Trump’s plan to push out federal employees by offering them financial incentives.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A short honeymoon for Catholics in D.C. as Vice President Vance clashes with bishops on migration
item.description: The sense of Catholic unity didn’t last long. Less than a week after Vice President JD Vance shared the inauguration stage with a senior Catholic cardinal, Vance picked a fight with the top American leaders of his church. The new vice president, a Catholic convert, accused the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With Gaza rehab and other global policy ideas, Trump goes from America First to America Everywhere
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump promised voters an administration that wouldn’t waste precious American lives and taxpayer treasure on far-off wars and nation building.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Callers swamp Senate offices in Trump's first weeks, leading to busy signals and full voicemail
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Callers are getting busy signals and voicemail inboxes are full at many U.S. Senate offices as people try to reach out and voice their opinions on President Donald Trump’s Cabinet picks, executive orders and moves to dismantle various federal programs.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration's push for a federal funding freeze is back in court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A planned Trump administration freeze on federal funding is heading back to a Washington courtroom on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Political parties explain Hillsborough County's conservative flip
item.description: As of Thursday, Hillsborough County records showed the number of active registered Republicans was bigger than the number of registered Democrats. The difference as of Friday was 307 voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
